rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use implementation annexes only for valid implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Ground all AGREE variables in the current AADL model or explicit declarations
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope to maintain architecture clarity
Preserve existing component connections and features when inserting new annex blocks
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when referring to component ports
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings like assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes when referring to component ports
Avoid encoding AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax for assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
